like veteran blacksmith.


THAT fellow is the best worker
that We have had at the rock pile
for months" said Jake Warner last
night. HE had a good head, too
and he used it, He could accom-
plish more in less time than any
other man on the chain gang and he
didn't have to work hard at that.
He was one of the few Industrial
Workers that was willing to work
and never caused any trouble. But
he certainly is wrapped up in his
faith. He came up here from Port
land during the switchmen's strike
He told the railroad authorities at
Portland that he wanted to take a
job here as a switchman and they
gave him a pass on the road. When
he got here the first thing he did
was to join the |. W. w. and get
arrested. I'm sorry to lose the
professor" because he certainly
did earn his $3.50 a day, although
it was applied on his fine"
